introSection: Riddler Mind Bender is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Georgia in Austell, Georgia, USA. The ride opened on March 31, 1978 and was advertised as the first "triple looping roller coaster". It was built by Schwarzkopf.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Mind Bender was advertised as the first "triple looping roller coaster"; however it only features two vertical loops. According to the park, the third is a "horizontal loop". This is actually a helix that dives into a ravine. It opened the same year as Shock Wave at Six Flags Over Texas, a similar looping roller coaster.

On June 17, 2008, American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride on its 30th anniversary as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark.

Following the 2019 season, refurbishment of the roller coaster began. Some sections of track were removed and replaced. The name was also changed from Mind Bender to The Riddler Mind Bender. New trains, with chassis and bogies built by Bolliger & Mabillard, were installed. The ride reopened on September 18, 2021.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The ride recieved new trains in 2021. These new trains where designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and built by Valente SAS. They are 2 cars shorter compared to the original Schwarzkopf trains, going from a 7x2x2 to a 5x2x2 arrangement.

fullText:
Riddler Mind Bender is a steel roller coaster located at Six Flags Over Georgia in Austell, Georgia, USA. The ride opened on March 31, 1978 and was advertised as the first "triple looping roller coaster". It was built by Schwarzkopf.

Mind Bender was advertised as the first "triple looping roller coaster"; however it only features two vertical loops. According to the park, the third is a "horizontal loop". This is actually a helix that dives into a ravine. It opened the same year as Shock Wave at Six Flags Over Texas, a similar looping roller coaster.

On June 17, 2008, American Coaster Enthusiasts recognized the ride on its 30th anniversary as an ACE Roller Coaster Landmark.

Following the 2019 season, refurbishment of the roller coaster began. Some sections of track were removed and replaced. The name was also changed from Mind Bender to The Riddler Mind Bender. New trains, with chassis and bogies built by Bolliger & Mabillard, were installed. The ride reopened on September 18, 2021.



Green track and supports.

The ride recieved new trains in 2021. These new trains where designed by Bolliger & Mabillard and built by Valente SAS. They are 2 cars shorter compared to the original Schwarzkopf trains, going from a 7x2x2 to a 5x2x2 arrangement.
